strengths: 15+ years of polyglot full-stack engineering with a continuous TypeScript/Node.js/React thread across re:cinq, Optilyz, and RIDE Capital, comfortably exceeding the 6-year minimum and demonstrating sustained senior-level output.

Active AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq — including MCP (Model Context Protocol) code generators and agentic workflow frameworks — is a direct, rare match for the role's AI agent development requirement.

Demonstrably deep testing culture: authored two open-source test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), ran repeated TDD/Code Kata workshops, and practiced strict TDD + trunk-based development at RIDE Capital, signalling the code-quality discipline the role requires.
concerns: Nest.js is not explicitly named anywhere in the resume; while strong Node.js/TypeScript experience covers the conceptual ground, hands-on Nest.js familiarity would need to be confirmed.
Kotlin is absent from the resume and Python does not appear in the skills list, leaving two of the required secondary languages unverified.

Relational database experience is underrepresented — MySQL appears in the skills list but PostgreSQL is not cited, and the most detailed DB work documented (Optilyz, GISCollective) centres on MongoDB.
response.justification: Bogdan is a strong fit for this role: his current position at re:cinq already combines the primary stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React) with AI-agent infrastructure work — the hardest requirement to find — and his 15-year history of rapidly adopting new languages and frameworks (D, Swift, Java, C++, GraphQL) makes the Nest.js and Kotlin gaps low-risk ramp-up items rather than structural blockers. His engineering rigour, evidenced by two published OSS test frameworks, recurring TDD workshops, and a micro-services background handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby, maps directly onto the role's architecture, quality, and performance responsibilities.
